name: hard2pow21-v2
description: Fixed hard instance: a deterministic 1,501,711-gate circuit (pads to 2^21). Generic arithmetic-heavy workload (Poseidon2 permutation chain), decoupled from any application. The circuit, witness, proof system, and config are PINNED; the only variable is the prover implementation.

fixedConfig.runtime: native (algorithm track: isolates the prover algorithm from platform effects like WASM codegen/SIMD)
fixedConfig.scheme: ultra_honk (poseidon2 oracle, ZK on — bb defaults)
fixedConfig.note: These are the rules of the task, not tunables. Single-threaded so the metric rewards algorithmic work, not parallelization. The proof system is frozen by the pinned baseline VK (problem/baseline_vk/vk): a candidate prover must produce proofs that the PINNED BASELINE verifier accepts against that VK.

baseline.bb: barretenberg built from aztec-packages branch `next` @ 7e94c2c0e3 (June 2026), pristine source, standard preset; on the canonical runner it is built by ci/setup.sh (clang-20, preset default); locally place a build at ~/.bb-next/bb or set BASELINE_BB
baseline.role: computes the pinned VK and verifies every candidate's proof (the trust boundary); also the reference row on both boards

officialEnvironment.runner: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04-arm (free for public repos; official-grade workflow)
officialEnvironment.cpu: Azure Cobalt 100 (Arm Neoverse N2), 4 vCPU, 16 GiB — homogeneous fleet
officialEnvironment.toolchain: clang-20 (apt.llvm.org), cmake preset default, single-thread via HARDWARE_CONCURRENCY=1

validity.soundness: baseline `bb verify` must accept the candidate's proof against the pinned VK
validity.freshWitness: a random input x is sampled per run (nargo execute on problem/source); the candidate's emitted public output must equal the expected output — kills cached/replayed proofs
validity.singleThread: bb log must report num threads: 1
validity.complete: prove must exit 0 and emit proof + public_inputs

budgets.rationale: Cross-budgets prevent trading one resource for rank on the other board: time entries must stay under the wasm32 4 GB ceiling (browser-transferable; blocks memory-for-speed blowups), memory entries must stay under 2x baseline time (127.0 = 2x the canonical-runner baseline median 63.67s; blocks recompute-everything wins). The disk-ops cap closes the mmap/spill loophole (file-backed pages hide from RSS; warm baseline does ~40 block-output ops — the proof files). The CRS must be pre-warmed (ci/setup.sh) or its one-time ~140MB download trips the disk gate.

grading.timeMetric: median wall-clock seconds of `bb prove` across runs (lower wins)
grading.memoryMetric: peak resident set size (MiB) of the prove process (lower wins)
grading.comparability: numbers are comparable only when graded on the same hardware; the canonical boards are produced by the official-grade CI workflow on the officialEnvironment runner. Local numbers are advisory.

v1_archive: Task v1 (bb v3.0.0-nightly.20260102, nargo beta.18, circuit 1501711 gates) boards archived in boards/archive-v1/; v2 regenerated the circuit/witness from problem/source with nargo beta.22 because next-format bb cannot parse v1 ACIR.
v2_macos_archive: Until 2026-06-10 the v2 boards were graded on the maintainer's Apple M-series (baseline 28.38s/2611MiB); those rows are archived in boards/archive-v2-macos/ and are not comparable with canonical-runner rows.
